# Questions in Livestream 20

Live Stream #20: What's Your Twenty?

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=243" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:03</a> | What is the relationship, if any, between the snake miracle in Exodus and the crocodile miracle in Papyrus Westcar? | He saw some broad similarity in the motif of an inanimate object becoming a dangerous creature, but not a close one-to-one dependence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=464" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">7:44</a> | Will he explain giants like Goliath in the ancient Near East, since literal giants are hard to believe? | He said he was still studying the issue and was not ready to speak definitively, though he was exploring relevant Egyptian material. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=738" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:18</a> | What are Behemoth and Leviathan in Job 40? | He leaned toward seeing them as mythic chaos creatures drawing on ancient Near Eastern creation-myth imagery rather than straightforward dinosaurs. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=976" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:16</a> | What does Zaphenath-paneah mean? | He argued for an Egyptian derivation tied to "the living one" rather than a later speculative explanation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=1082" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:02</a> | Why is Shishak named directly in Kings and Chronicles instead of just being called Pharaoh? | He said he did not know for sure, but suggested it may reflect the way Shishak's reign and later memory were framed in the biblical tradition. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=1408" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:28</a> | In Psalm 4, what was the musician supposed to do with headings like "to the chief musician" and "on neginoth"? | He said the headings are performance instructions: the psalm was meant to be set and delivered musically, likely with stringed accompaniment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=1637" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:17</a> | Could the *mache* in Sinai 346 be connected to biblical Moses as some writers claim? | He said no convincing case has been made. He strongly criticized the epigraphy behind that argument. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=1847" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:47</a> | Does he think we will find the tomb of Herihor, or another royal cache or intact royal tomb? | He hoped so. He said more royal burials may still be found, and thought Herihor may have been buried somewhere near Thebes rather than in the Valley of the Kings itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=2109" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:09</a> | Is the Deir el-Bahri cache more important than Tutankhamun's tomb? | He said no: the Deir el-Bahri cache is very important, but Tutankhamun's tomb was the greater game-changing discovery. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=2349" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:09</a> | Does he know any Assyriologists doing the same kind of Bible-and-ancient-context work on YouTube, and do Assyriologists get along with him? | He named Alan Millard and Paul Lawrence as scholars doing related work, said he knows few academics doing this on YouTube, and said some Assyriologists get along with him and some do not. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=2629" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">43:49</a> | What does he think about astrotheology and claims that the Bible encodes constellations or heavenly symbolism? | He said some analogical heavenly symbolism is real, but he has serious problems with expansive astrotheological systems. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=2934" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">48:54</a> | What is his opinion of John Romer? | He said Romer is an unusual throwback figure with some unorthodox ideas; he respects parts of the work but does not treat him as a conventional academic model. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=3343" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:43</a> | What source should people use for biblical numerology? | He recommended caution and said he would start with solid commentaries on particular biblical books rather than numerology systems. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=3554" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:14</a> | What does he think about spoken-language immersion programs for ancient Greek? | He liked the method in principle and thought language-acquisition approaches can be very helpful. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=3820" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:03:40</a> | Why does Eden have temple imagery, and did the fall of man happen in an actual temple? | He said Eden represents sacred space with God, humanity, and cherubim; the fall did not occur in a literal built temple, but later temples recreate that creation imagery. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=4125" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:08:45</a> | Which Ptolemaic king requested the Septuagint, if it was commissioned at all? | He said the traditional story is debated, likely concerns an early Ptolemy, and he was not sure whether it should be assigned to Ptolemy II or Ptolemy III without checking the letter again. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=4303" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:43</a> | Were there vampire myths or similar ideas in Egypt, and what about the evil eye or goat-demon parallels? | He said no real vampire myth is known, though one Pyramid Text has blood-drinking imagery; Egypt did have "bad eye" magic, but not a close goat-demon equivalent. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=4529" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:29</a> | How should Michael Heiser's divine council language be handled without compromising monotheism? | He said the concern is real and preferred speaking of those beings as created spiritual beings rather than gods in any strong sense. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=4934" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:14</a> | Can he talk about households, household gods, and their significance? | He said household structures varied by culture, and household gods were common across the ancient Near East, including Egypt, where homes could contain niches for domestic devotion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=5240" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:20</a> | What did Egyptians believe sphinxes were, and what were they protecting and from what? | He said royal sphinxes embodied royal power and were shown as protecting Egypt, its king, and its food supply from foreign enemies. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=5419" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:19</a> | If he holds minority views, how can people tell they have merit and are not just fringe claims like creationist arguments? | He said his view is a minority position within biblical studies rather than archaeology, and argued it remains tied to credentialed scholars, evidence, and academic method. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=5728" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:35:28</a> | Can documentary-hypothesis models support inspiration, or do they damage biblical credibility? | He said attempts to reconcile strong documentary models with inspiration usually fail because endless redaction tends to undermine confidence in inspired originals. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=5966" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:26</a> | Can he explain his objections to the documentary hypothesis in more detail? | He objected to its source criteria, saying divine-name shifts, voice changes, narrative-poetry pairing, and other features are not reliable source divisions when compared with other ancient Near Eastern texts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=6486" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:48:06</a> | Is it true that Herod the Great had a rivalry with Cleopatra, and if so to what extent? | He said there was a rivalry, but cautioned that first-century BC history is outside his specialty and he did not want to press the extent beyond that. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=6606" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:06</a> | Why is the documentary hypothesis so popular in North America? | He said North American theological education can be insular and skeptical in ways that help the theory reproduce itself institutionally. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UR7cGZCgB3E?t=7065" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:45</a> | What are his thoughts on Egypt during the end times based on Isaiah 19 and Daniel 11:40-45? | He said much of Isaiah 19 was already fulfilled, but the closing restoration language may have eschatological significance; in Daniel 11, he identified the king of the south with Egypt in ancient Israelite geographic terms. |
